km_name: SpecificTopic Binary KM - Consumers, End-Users, Product Safety and Privacy
purpose: Binary Yes/No SpecificTopic decisions for the consumers_products_privacy family. This simplified KM owns its tag-level reconciliation and emits only final Yes tags for this family.

rules: Answer every tag in this KM independently as Yes or No before producing the family final list.
Emit only tags answered Yes in final_specific_topics_from_this_family.
Do not emit candidates, centrality labels, or nearby labels as final tags.
If two tags overlap, keep both only when the source has distinct operative evidence for both; otherwise answer Yes only for the more directly satisfied decision question.
Reject incidental, background, implementation-actor-only, and keyword-only evidence even when a semantic signal appears.
The standalone SpecificTopic reconciler has been removed; this family KM is responsible for suppressing weak/adjacent labels before code consolidation.
weak_evidence_policy: Weak or not-observed tags remain in the enum, but need direct source evidence at runtime.
Do not use weak master examples as independent proof; use them only as cautionary boundary tests.
weak_or_not_observed_tags_in_this_family: Personal Safety of Non-Consumers and End-Users
Social Inclusion of Consumers and End-Users

tag_decisions.tag: canonical SpecificTopic value from this KM
tag_decisions.evidence_excerpt: required when answer is Yes; empty string allowed when No
tag_decisions.nearby_label_if_any: optional canonical tag that better fits the evidence

final_specific_topics_from_this_family: all tags from tag_decisions with answer=Yes, already reconciled by this family KM

tags.definition: Safety requirements, risk controls, conformity, market surveillance, warnings, or liability for goods/services placed on the market.
tags.decision_question: Should this source be tagged SpecificTopic = "Product Safety"?
yes_when: The regulated object is product safety, defects, safety standards, toys, cosmetics, food safety, machinery/product risks, or market surveillance.
There is a source-supported evidence role: regulated_object, legal_mechanism, protected_interest, direct_addressee, direct_regulated_product/facility, or final-topic ESRS mapping trace.
The tag is primary, companion, or benchmark_expected_companion; it is not merely adjacent or incidental.
no_when: The source regulates product labeling, chemical registration, or consumer rights without safety-risk controls.
The only support is a keyword hit, source URL/domain/slug, filename, row ID, benchmark answer, generic legal boilerplate, or implementation/enforcement actor.
A narrower or different canonical label captures the operative source evidence more accurately.

test_case.question: A source's title/scope/operative provisions centrally say: "a product is defective when it does not provide the safety which a person is entitled to expect". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Product Safety"? Answer Yes/No and cite the evidence role.
test_case.reasoning_check: Yes if the quoted evidence satisfies the tag_when rule: The regulated object is product safety, defects, safety standards, toys, cosmetics, food safety, machinery/product risks, or market surveillance.
forces_reasoning_about: whether the excerpt is source evidence rather than a benchmark artifact

test_case.question: A source mentions "product safety" once in background, but its operative duties regulate a different subject and no duty matches "The regulated object is product safety, defects, safety standards, toys, cosmetics, food safety, machinery/product risks, or market surveillance.". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Product Safety"?
test_case.reasoning_check: No. A keyword or background reference is not enough; the label needs source-supported central evidence.

test_case.question: A source fits this exclusion: "The source regulates product labeling, chemical registration, or consumer rights without safety-risk controls.". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Product Safety"?
test_case.reasoning_check: No. The label-specific do_not_tag_when rule excludes this case.

test_case.question: The master evidence includes a weak/indirect edge example: "market surveillance means the activities carried out and measures taken by public authorities to ensure that EEE complies with the requirements set out in this Directive and does not endanger health, safety or other issues of public interest protection". Standing alone, should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Product Safety"?
test_case.reasoning_check: The master reason was classified as weak/cautionary; the LLM must look for central legal duties, not rely on weak contextual association.

tags.definition: Conformity assessment, certification, licensing, accreditation, or compliance marks demonstrating that products or organizations meet requirements.
tags.decision_question: Should this source be tagged SpecificTopic = "Compliance and Certification"?
yes_when: The source centrally requires certificates, conformity assessment, accreditation, certification bodies, compliance marks, or similar formal verification.
There is a source-supported evidence role: regulated_object, legal_mechanism, protected_interest, direct_addressee, direct_regulated_product/facility, or final-topic ESRS mapping trace.
The tag is primary, companion, or benchmark_expected_companion; it is not merely adjacent or incidental.
no_when: The source creates legal duties but no certification/conformity process; do not tag solely for general compliance.
The only support is a keyword hit, source URL/domain/slug, filename, row ID, benchmark answer, generic legal boilerplate, or implementation/enforcement actor.
A narrower or different canonical label captures the operative source evidence more accurately.

tags.definition: Central rules protecting consumers in transactions, marketing, contracts, products, services, information asymmetry, financial services, or unfair trader practices.
tags.decision_question: Should this source be tagged SpecificTopic = "Consumer Protection Laws"?
yes_when: The source centrally protects consumers/end-users through rights, remedies, unfair commercial-practice rules, misleading-advertising controls, contract safeguards, product/service information, or consumer transaction protections.
There is a source-supported evidence role: regulated_object, legal_mechanism, protected_interest, direct_addressee, direct_regulated_product/facility, or final-topic ESRS mapping trace.
The tag is primary, companion, or benchmark_expected_companion; it is not merely adjacent or incidental.
no_when: Do not tag where consumers are only indirectly affected by environmental, labor, industrial, product-stewardship, or reporting rules. For downstream ESRS mapping, distinguish market/trader conduct from direct end-user safety or information impacts.
The only support is a keyword hit, source URL/domain/slug, filename, row ID, benchmark answer, generic legal boilerplate, or implementation/enforcement actor.
A narrower or different canonical label captures the operative source evidence more accurately.

Evidence-packet support for this tag is weak, indirect, or absent; answer Yes only when the current source text independently and directly satisfies the definition, decision question, and a yes_when rule.
no_when: The source is only about labeling, recall, waste disposal, or consumer rights after sale.
The only support is a keyword hit, source URL/domain/slug, filename, row ID, benchmark answer, generic legal boilerplate, or implementation/enforcement actor.
